content.text: Do seven 7 and only seven of the following. In the following \(D^2\) is the closed, two-dimensional disk, and \(S^1\) is the unit circle, its boundary. The cyclic group of order \(n\), is \(\mathbb{Z}_n=\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}\).

body.text: Assume that \(X\) is completely regular. Show that \(X\) is connected if and only if \(\beta X\) is connected.

subparts.label: a.
subparts.text: Define a retract and show there is no retract \(D^2\to S^1\).

subparts.label: b.
subparts.text: Show that any continuous map \(f:D^2\to D^2\) has a fixed point.

subparts.label: c.
subparts.text: If \(A\) is retract of \(D^2\) prove that any continuous map \(f:A\to A\) has a fixed point.

body.text:
Let \(M\) and \(N\) be two connected, compact manifolds with points \(x_0\in M\) and \(y_0\in N\). Define an equivalence relation on their disjoint union, \(M\sqcup N\), as \(x_0\sim y_0\) and all other points are just equivalent to themselves. Let \(M\vee N=(M\sqcup N)/{\sim}\). Show that
\[
\pi_1(M\vee N,[x_0])=\pi_1(M,x_0)*\pi_1(N,y_0)
\]
where \([x_0]\) is the equivalence class of \(x_0\).
Construct a space whose fundamental group is isomorphic to \((\mathbb{Z}\oplus\mathbb{Z}_3)*\mathbb{Z}_5\).
Show that \(S^2\) is not homeomorphic to \(S^n\) for any \(n>2\).
State the Seifert–van Kampen Theorem and use it to compute the fundamental group of the space pictured below.

body.alt: A two-lobed surface crossing at its center. The left lobe contains a small oval hole, and the right lobe has a transverse circular cross-section whose hidden portion is dashed.

body.text: The projective plane \(PP\) is constructed by identifying the edges of a disk as pictured below. Compute the fundamental group of \(PP\) as well as that of \(PP\) minus \(n\) points, \(n>0\).

body.alt: A lens-shaped disk with its upper and lower boundary arcs both labeled a. An arrow on the upper arc points right, and an arrow on the lower arc points left, indicating the edge identification.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define a deformation retract and show it induces an isomorphism on fundamental groups.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove or disprove: a retract induces an isomorphism on fundamental groups.
